title: Solving Rotational Indeterminacy
text: fit Bayesian EFA model using befa(). illustrate pathology rotational indeterminacy, explicitly disable internal alignment process setting rotate = "none". see , attempting summarize posterior distributions stage meaningless due rotational indeterminacy. applying post-hoc alignment rotation method, MCMC chains freely explored unidentified, multimodal parameter space, generating several obvious anomalies summaries: Null summaries: Means medians collapse toward zero equivalent rotations opposing signs cancel . Extreme uncertainty: Posterior SDs credible intervals become massive (e.g., spanning -0.8 +0.8), rendering estimates uninformative. Poor convergence diagnostics: lack identification causes unstable convergence, reflected \(\hat{R}\) values 1.00 low effective sample sizes, indicated output warning. visual inspection posterior draws specific parameter (e.g., Lambda[1,2]) makes severity unidentified parameter space immediately apparent:  Instead clean, unimodal distribution centered around true value \(0.7\) (true simulated value), bimodal shape caused sampler traversing different orthogonal rotations.

title: Solving Rotational Indeterminacy
text: align results, pass raw MCMC matrix rsp_align(). Since samplers flatten multidimensional parameters (like loading matrix \(\mathbf{\Lambda}\)) vectors, function requires specific formatting: lambda_draws: numeric matrix \(S \times (J \times M)\), \(S\) total number draws, \(J\) number items, \(M\) number factors. n_chains: number MCMC chains. function assumes rows lambda_draws stacked chain chain (e.g., 4000 draws 4 chains, rows 1-1000 belong chain 1, rows 1001-2000 chain 2, etc.). format: critical argument; specifies MCMC software flattened original \(J \times M\) loading matrix single row.

title: Solving Rotational Indeterminacy
text: lambda_draws input must 2D matrix dimensions \(S \times (J \times M)\). Since MCMC samplers flatten original \(J \times M\) loading matrix \(\mathbf{\Lambda}\) single row vector, format argument tells function reconstruct . Consider \(4 \times 3\) loading matrix (4 items, 3 factors): \[ \mathbf{\Lambda} = \begin{bmatrix} \lambda_{11} & \lambda_{12} & \lambda_{13} \\ \lambda_{21} & \lambda_{22} & \lambda_{23} \\ \lambda_{31} & \lambda_{32} & \lambda_{33} \\ \lambda_{41} & \lambda_{42} & \lambda_{43} \end{bmatrix} \] Depending MCMC software, input rows must follow one two structures: Column-major (format = "column_major"): Elements filled factor factor. default output Stan matches .vector(Lambda) behavior R: \[ \text{vec}(\mathbf{\Lambda}) = [ \underbrace{\lambda_{11}, \lambda_{21}, \lambda_{31}, \lambda_{41}}_{\text{Factor 1}}, \, \underbrace{\lambda_{12}, \lambda_{22}, \lambda_{32}, \lambda_{42}}_{\text{Factor 2}}, \, \underbrace{\lambda_{13}, \lambda_{23}, \lambda_{33}, \lambda_{43}}_{\text{Factor 3}} ] \] Row-major (format = "row_major"): Elements filled item item. corresponds .vector(t(Lambda)) R: \[ \text{vec}(\mathbf{\Lambda}^\top) = [ \underbrace{\lambda_{11}, \lambda_{12}, \lambda_{13}}_{\text{Item 1}}, \, \underbrace{\lambda_{21}, \lambda_{22}, \lambda_{23}}_{\text{Item 2}}, \, \underbrace{\lambda_{31}, \lambda_{32}, \lambda_{33}}_{\text{Item 3}}, \, \underbrace{\lambda_{41}, \lambda_{42}, \lambda_{43}}_{\text{Item 4}} ] \] can easily visualize difference looking column names extracted draws. \(6 \times 2\) loading matrix:
code: # 1. Draws in column-major order (Stan's default) # Notice how it fills Factor 1 completely before moving to Factor 2 colnames(draws_unaligned) #>  [1] "Lambda[1,1]" "Lambda[2,1]" "Lambda[3,1]" "Lambda[4,1]" "Lambda[5,1]" #>  [6] "Lambda[6,1]" "Lambda[1,2]" "Lambda[2,2]" "Lambda[3,2]" "Lambda[4,2]" #> [11] "Lambda[5,2]" "Lambda[6,2]"  # 2. Draws in row-major order # We can simulate this order by transposing the index matrix rw_ord <- rep(1:6, each = 2) + rep(c(0, 6), times = 2) colnames(draws_unaligned)[rw_ord] #>  [1] "Lambda[1,1]" "Lambda[1,2]" "Lambda[2,1]" "Lambda[2,2]" "Lambda[3,1]" #>  [6] "Lambda[3,2]" "Lambda[4,1]" "Lambda[4,2]" "Lambda[5,1]" "Lambda[5,2]" #> [11] "Lambda[6,1]" "Lambda[6,2]"

title: Solving Rotational Indeterminacy
text: Now correct format, can apply alignment algorithm. example (6 items, 2 factors, 4 chains), use column_major format match extraction tool: aligning posterior draws, rotational indeterminacy fully resolved. previously observed anomalies disappear, true factor structure emerges high precision clarity: Clear Factor Structure: previous non-significant loadings replaced perfect simple structure. Items 1–3 now load onto Factor 2, \(\lambda \approx .68\) across items, Items 4–6 load onto Factor 1, loadings ranging \(0.66\) \(0.80\). High precision: Posterior standard deviations decreased significantly, \(\approx 0.50\) \(0.04\), resulting narrow, informative credible intervals clearly exclude zero main loadings. Perfect convergence mixing: model now identified, MCMC diagnostics perfect. \(\hat{R}\) values stabilized \(1.00\), effective sample sizes (ESS) increased significantly (well 2000), confirming efficient mixing stable chains. Re-visualizing posterior distribution Lambda[1,1] alignment provides clear visual proof parameter now identified. formerly erratic, multimodal draws successfully resolved clean, unimodal distribution, confirming rotational indeterminacy completely eliminated.

title: Getting Started with BayesEFA
text: BayesEFA provides robust intuitive framework Bayesian Exploratory Factor Analysis using Stan. Unconstraint Estimation: Fit unrestricted loading matrices without fixing parameters using arbitrary identification constraints. Resolved Rotational Indeterminacy: Recover interpretable posteriors Efficient Rotation-Sign-Permutation (E-RSP) alignment algorithm. Bayesian SEM Fit Measures: Includes Bayesian SEM fit indices proposed Garnier-Villarreal & Jorgensen (2020) model evaluation. Full Posterior Inference: Obtain complete distributions quantities, including factor loadings, factor scores, fit measures, reliability indices. FIML Missing Data: Handles incomplete datasets via Full Information Maximum Likelihood (FIML). Inherent Robustness: Naturally resolves common frequentist issues, Heywood cases non-positive definite matrices, ensuring stable estimation. package supports rstan cmdstanr, rstan used default backend. However, strongly recommend using cmdstanr users prefer latest Stan features faster estimation, cmdstanr can installed following official guide. Note: BayesEFA model compiles first using cmdstanr. permanently saved system’s cache persist across R sessions computer reboots. ever need force recompile (e.g., updating cmdstanr), must manually clear cache running: unlink(tools::R_user_dir("BayesEFA", = "cache"), recursive = TRUE).

title: Getting Started with BayesEFA
text: following workflow fits 3-factor model using befa() function, handling estimation, rotation, post-processing single step: befa_fit object contains standard stanfit object (rstan). ensures full compatibility Stan ecosystem, allowing use favorite diagnostic tools plots—regardless whether used cmdstanr rstan backend.
code: library(BayesEFA)  # Fit Bayesian EFA model befa_fit <- befa(   data = HS_data,                     # Data   n_factors = 3,                      # Nº of latent factors   model = "cor",                      # Model the correlation matrix   lambda_prior = "unit_vector",       # Unit-vector prior (Rey-Sáez et al., 2025)   rotate = "varimax",                 # Automatic Varimax + E-RSP Alignment   backend = "rstan",                  # Estimation backend, also "cmdstanr"   factor_scores = TRUE,               # Compute Bayesian factor scores   compute_fit_indices = TRUE,         # Compute Bayesian SEM fit indices   compute_reliability = TRUE,         # Compute reliability indices   iter_sampling = 1000,               # Sampling iterations   iter_warmup = 1000,                 # Warmup iterations   chains = 4,                         # 4 MCMC chains   parallel_chains = 1,                # no parallel chains   seed = 17                           # seed for reproducible results )

title: Bayesian Exploratory Factor Analysis — befa
code: befa(   data = NULL,   n_factors = NULL,   ordered = FALSE,   sample_nobs = NULL,   sample_mean = NULL,   sample_cov = NULL,   sample_cor = NULL,   model = c("cor", "cov", "raw"),   lambda_prior = c("unit_vector", "normal"),   missing = c("listwise", "FIML"),   rotate = c("varimax", "none"),   rsp_args = NULL,   loo_args = NULL,   factor_scores = FALSE,   backend = c("rstan", "cmdstanr"),   prior = list(),   compute_fit_indices = TRUE,   compute_reliability = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Bayesian Exploratory Factor Analysis — befa
text: data Numeric matrix data.frame (N rows, J columns). Raw observations. Mutually exclusive sample_cov/sample_cor. n_factors Integer. Number latent factors extract. ordered Logical. TRUE, fits ordinal model. Currently implemented. sample_nobs Integer. Sample size. Required using summary statistics instead data. sample_mean Numeric vector (length J). Sample means. Used model="raw" summary stats. sample_cov Numeric matrix (J x J). Sample covariance matrix. Mutually exclusive data. sample_cor Numeric matrix (J x J). Sample correlation matrix. Mutually exclusive data. model Character. Model parameterization: "cor" (default): Estimates model-implied correlation matrix. "cov": Estimates model-implied covariance matrix. "raw": Full model. Estimates model-implied mean-vector covariance matrix. lambda_prior Character. Prior family loadings: "unit_vector" (default): Unit-vector prior distribution proposed Rey-Sáez et al., 2025 (see details) "normal": Independent normal priors factor loading. Normal priors available model="cor". missing Character. Method handling missing data: "listwise" (default): Complete-case analysis (removes rows NA). "FIML": Full Information Maximum Likelihood. Uses available data, estimating parameters using observed data pattern case. Requires raw data. rotate Character. Rotation criterion post-processing: "varimax" (default): Apply Varimax rotation RSP alignment. "none": rotation alignment applied. rsp_args List. Arguments efficient RSP alignment algorithm: max_iter: Maximum iterations (default 1000). threshold: Convergence threshold (default 1e-6). loo_args List. Arguments passed loo::loo() leave-one-cross-validation compute_fit_indices = TRUE. Common arguments include: cores: Number cores use parallelization. r_eff: Logical. TRUE, computes relative effective sample sizes. factor_scores Logical. TRUE, computes stores factor scores. Requires data. backend Character. Stan interface: "rstan" (default) "cmdstanr". prior Named list. User-defined prior hyperparameters (override defaults): xi: Repulsion parameter (unit_vector prior). Default: 100. recommended modified user. nu: c(mean, sd) intercepts (raw model). Default: c(0, 40) -> N(0, 40). sigma: c(df, loc, scale) residual SDs (cov/raw models). what: Bayesian Fit Indices for Exploratory Factor Analysis — befa_fit_measures
title: Bayesian Fit Indices for Exploratory Factor Analysis — befa_fit_measures
text: Computes Bayesian analogs common Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) fit indices. implementation follows DevM framework proposed Garnier-Villarreal Jorgensen (2020), translates traditional maximum-likelihood fit indices Bayesian framework. evaluating indices across MCMC draws, function yields full posterior distributions, allowing quantification sampling uncertainty (e.g., 95% credible intervals) rather relying single point estimate.

text: object befa object returned befa(). ... Additional arguments passed Stan automatically fitting null model (e.g., iter, chains, warmup, loo_args).

title: Bayesian Fit Indices for Exploratory Factor Analysis — befa_fit_measures
text: list class befa_fitmeasures containing: fit_draws: draws_array (iterations \(\times\) chains \(\times\) P) containing posterior draws fit indices (Chi2, Chi2_Null, BRMSEA, BGamma, Adj_BGamma, BMc, SRMR, BCFI, BTLI). summary: draws_summary data frame computed via posterior::summarise_draws(). posterior_fit: data frame containing full MCMC posterior draws \(\chi^2\)-based indices, enabling custom plotting density estimation. loo_object: full loo object returned loo::loo(). details: list internal model constants (\(p^*\), \(pD\), \(N\), chi2_ppp).

title: Bayesian Fit Indices for Exploratory Factor Analysis — befa_fit_measures
text: DevM Framework (Deviance Posterior Mean) frequentist SEM, fit indices derived exact fit test statistic (\(\chi^2\)) model degrees freedom (\(df\)). Bayesian DevM framework (Garnier-Villarreal & Jorgensen, 2020), quantities replaced Bayesian analogs: Observed Deviance (\(D_i^{obs}\)): model deviance evaluated posterior draw \(\), serving Bayesian analog \(\chi^2\). Effective Number Parameters (\(pD\)): penalty model complexity. Following authors' recommendations, function estimates \(pD\) using Leave-One-Information Criterion (\(p_{loo}\)) via Pareto Smoothed Importance Sampling Leave-One-(PSIS-LOO, Vehtari et al., 2017). Saturated Moments (\(p^*\)): number nonredundant sample moments (e.g., \(J \cdot (J + 3) / 2\) raw data, \(J \cdot (J + 1) / 2\) covariance matrices). Bayesian Degrees Freedom (\(df_B\)): Defined \(df_B = p^* - pD\). Bayesian Noncentrality Parameter (\(\lambda_i\)): Defined iteration \(\lambda_i = D_i^{obs} - p^*\). Absolute Fit Indices Absolute indices evaluate well hypothesized model reproduces observed data. function computes following indices draw \(\): BRMSEA (Bayesian Root Mean Square Error Approximation): $$\text{BRMSEA}_i = \sqrt{\max [0, (D_i^{obs} - p^*) / (df_B \cdot N)]}$$ BGamma (\(\hat{\Gamma}\)) Adjusted BGamma (\(\hat{\Gamma}_{adj}\)): Bayesian analogs GFI. $$\hat{\Gamma}_i = J / [J + (2 / N) \cdot (D_i^{obs} - p^*)]$$ $$\hat{\Gamma}_{adj,} = 1 - (p^* / df_B) \cdot (1 - \hat{\Gamma}_i)$$ BMc (Bayesian McDonald's Centrality Index): $$\text{BMc}_i = \exp [-(1 / (2 \cdot N)) \cdot (D_i^{obs} - p^*)]$$ Chi2_ppp (Posterior Predictive P-value): proportion MCMC iterations deviance replicated data exceeds observed deviance \(P(D^{rep} \geq D^{obs})\). Values closer 0.50 indicate excellent fit, values \(< .05\) suggest poor fit. SRMR: Standardized Root Mean Square Residual, computed explicitly MCMC draw. Incremental Fit Indices Incremental indices compare hypothesized model (\(M_H\)) worst-case baseline independence model (\(M_0\)), observed variables assumed uncorrelated. Note: function automatically estimates null model hood using Stan settings, may increase computation time. BCFI (Bayesian Comparative Fit Index): $$\text{BCFI}_i = 1 - [(D_{H,}^{obs} - p^*) / (D_{0,}^{obs} - p^*)]$$ BTLI (Bayesian Tucker-Lewis Index / Non-Normed Fit Index): $$\text{BTLI}_i = [ (D_{0,}^{obs} - p^*) / df_{B0} - (D_{H,}^{obs} - p^*) / df_{BH} ] / [ (D_{0,}^{obs} - p^*) / df_{B0} - 1 ]$$ (Note: BCFI BTLI strictly bounded \([0, 1]\) interval final output). Predictive Fit Indices --sample predictive accuracy assessed using PSIS-LOO cross-validation: ELPD: Expected Log Pointwise Predictive Density. LOOIC: Leave-One-Information Criterion (\(-2 \cdot \text{ELPD}\)). p_loo: Estimated effective number parameters (\(pD\)).

title: Bayesian Reliability Estimation (McDonald's Omega) — befa_reliability
text: Unlike Cronbach's \(\alpha\), strictly assumes tau-equivalence (equal factor loadings across items), McDonald's \(\omega\) correctly accounts congeneric factor structure estimated Exploratory Factor Analysis (EFA). Furthermore, computing coefficients draw--draw posterior distribution, function naturally propagates parameter uncertainty, yielding full credible intervals reliability estimates.

title: Bayesian Reliability Estimation (McDonald's Omega) — befa_reliability
text: Let \(J\) number items, \(M\) number factors, \(\mathbf{\Lambda}\) \(J \times M\) loading matrix, \(\mathbf{\Psi}\) diagonal matrix unique variances (uniquenesses), \(\mathbf{\Sigma}\) model-implied covariance matrix. \(\mathbf{1}\) vector ones length \(J\). Omega Total (\(\omega_t\)): Represents proportion variance unit-weighted total score (sum items) attributable common factors. $$\omega_t = \frac{\sum_{m=1}^M (\sum_{j=1}^J \lambda_{jm})^2}{\mathbf{1}^\top \cdot \mathbf{\Sigma} \cdot \mathbf{1}}$$ Omega Subscale (\(\omega_{s,m}\)): Represents reliability sum score isolating variance attributable specific factor \(m\), treating unique variances measurement error. $$\omega_{s,m} = \frac{(\sum_{j=1}^J \lambda_{jm})^2}{(\sum_{j=1}^J \lambda_{jm})^2 + \sum_{j=1}^J \psi_j}$$

Lambda_star: \(J \times M\) numeric matrix representing final reference configuration (posterior mean aligned draws). objective: numeric value indicating final alignment objective (total Frobenius discrepancy across draws). sign_vectors: matrix dimensions \(S \times M\) containing sign flips applied draw. perm_vectors: matrix dimensions \(S \times M\) containing permutations applied draw. summary: draws_summary data frame computed via posterior::summarise_draws().

title: Efficient Rotation-Sign-Permutation (E-RSP) Alignment — rsp_align
text: Alignment Necessary Factor models invariant orthogonal transformations: \(\mathbf{\Lambda}\) valid loading matrix, \(\mathbf{\Lambda}^\bullet = \mathbf{\Lambda} \cdot \mathbf{Q}\) yields identical model-implied covariance matrix orthogonal matrix \(\mathbf{Q}\) \(\mathbf{Q} \cdot \mathbf{Q}^\top = \mathbf{}_M\). MCMC sampling, invariance creates multimodal posterior sampler explores equivalent, yet incompatible, rotational orientations. Consequently, naive posterior summaries (averaging across draws) mix modes, yielding uninterpretable loading estimates artificially cancel toward zero. Efficient RSP Algorithm (E-RSP) function implements Efficient Rotation-Sign-Permutation (E-RSP) algorithm (Rey-Sáez et al., 2026), optimized version exact RSP method originally proposed Papastamoulis Ntzoufras (2022). original approach becomes computationally prohibitive number latent factors increases, E-RSP reduces alignment task Linear Assignment Problem (LAP). guarantees globally optimal solution scales high-dimensional models negligible computational cost. algorithm proceeds two stages: Continuous Alignment (Varimax): posterior draw rotated canonical simple structure fix continuous rotational degree freedom. Discrete Alignment (Signed-Permutation): remaining discrete ambiguity (column permutations sign flips) resolved mapping draw common reference configuration. Flattening Formats lambda_draws input must 2D matrix dimensions S \(\times\) (J \(\times\) M), row represents flattened loading matrix single MCMC draw. format argument simply tells function MCMC software flattened original \(J \times M\) loading matrix \(\mathbf{\Lambda}\) row vector. aligned output returned exact flattened format. illustrate, consider \(4 \times 3\) loading matrix (4 items, 3 factors): $$\mathbf{\Lambda} = \begin{bmatrix} \lambda_{11} & \lambda_{12} & \lambda_{13} \\ \lambda_{21} & \lambda_{22} & \lambda_{23} \\ \lambda_{31} & \lambda_{32} & \lambda_{33} \\ \lambda_{41} & \lambda_{42} & \lambda_{43} \end{bmatrix}$$ Depending format specified, input rows must structured follows: Column-major (format = "column_major"): Elements filled column column (factor factor). corresponds .vector(Lambda) behavior R default output layout Stan: $$\text{vec}(\mathbf{\Lambda}) = \big[ \underbrace{\lambda_{11}, \lambda_{21}, \lambda_{31}, \lambda_{41}}_{\text{Factor 1}}, \, \underbrace{\lambda_{12}, \lambda_{22}, \lambda_{32}, \lambda_{42}}_{\text{Factor 2}}, \, \underbrace{\lambda_{13}, \lambda_{23}, \lambda_{33}, \lambda_{43}}_{\text{Factor 3}} \big]$$ Row-major (format = "row_major"): Elements filled row row (item item). corresponds .vector(t(Lambda)) R: $$\text{vec}(\mathbf{\Lambda}^\top) = \big[ \underbrace{\lambda_{11}, \lambda_{12}, \lambda_{13}}_{\text{Item 1}}, \, \underbrace{\lambda_{21}, \lambda_{22}, \lambda_{23}}_{\text{Item 2}}, \, \underbrace{\lambda_{31}, \lambda_{32}, \lambda_{33}}_{\text{Item 3}}, \, \underbrace{\lambda_{41}, \lambda_{42}, \lambda_{43}}_{\text{Item 4}} \big]$$

title: Efficient Rotation-Sign-Permutation (E-RSP) Alignment — rsp_align
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Fit Bayesian EFA model befa_fit <- befa(   data = HS_data,   n_factors = 3,   rotate = "none",   factor_scores = FALSE,   compute_fit_indices = FALSE,   compute_reliability = FALSE,   backend = "rstan",   seed = 17,   chains = 4,   parallel_chains = 4 )  # Extract unrotated posterior draws lambda_unrotated <- extract_posterior_draws(befa_fit, pars = "Lambda")  # See multimodality due to rotational indeterminacy hist(lambda_unrotated[, 1],   breaks = 100, col = "steelblue2",   main = "Rotation indeterminacy", xlab = "Lambda[1,1]" )  # See that columns are ordered following a column-major order # see Details on rsp_align function colnames(lambda_unrotated)  #  [1] "Lambda[1,1]" "Lambda[2,1]" "Lambda[3,1]" "Lambda[4,1]" "Lambda[5,1]" "Lambda[6,1]" "Lambda[7,1]" #  [8] "Lambda[8,1]" "Lambda[9,1]" "Lambda[1,2]" "Lambda[2,2]" "Lambda[3,2]" "Lambda[4,2]" "Lambda[5,2]" # [15] "Lambda[6,2]" "Lambda[7,2]" "Lambda[8,2]" "Lambda[9,2]" "Lambda[1,3]" "Lambda[2,3]" "Lambda[3,3]" # [22] "Lambda[4,3]" "Lambda[5,3]" "Lambda[6,3]" "Lambda[7,3]" "Lambda[8,3]" "Lambda[9,3]"  # Now, let's align posterior draws lambda_aligned <- rsp_align(   lambda_draws = lambda_unrotated,   n_items = ncol(HS_data),   n_factors = 3,   n_chains = 4,   format = "column_major" )  # Now, rotational indeterminacy has been solved! hist(lambda_aligned$Lambda_hat_mcmc[, 1],   breaks = 100, col = "steelblue2",   main = "Aligned posterior distribution", xlab = "Lambda[1,1]" ) } # }

title: BayesEFA 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release.

title: BayesEFA 0.1.0
text: Bayesian Exploratory Factor Analysis via Hamiltonian Monte Carlo (Stan). Three model parameterizations: correlation ("cor"), covariance ("cov"), full unstandardized ("raw"). Unit-vector prior factor loadings ensuring valid variance partitioning Heywood case prevention (Rey-Sáez et al., 2025). RSP alignment algorithm Varimax rotation resolving rotational indeterminacy across MCMC draws. Full Information Maximum Likelihood (FIML) missing data handling. Bayesian fit indices: BRMSEA, BCFI, BTLI, SRMR, Chi-squared posterior predictive p-value, PSIS-LOO cross-validation. Omega reliability coefficients (total subscale) full posterior uncertainty quantification. Bayesian factor scores posterior distributions. Dual backend support: rstan cmdstanr. Efficient C++ implementations (RcppArmadillo) RSP algorithm factor score computation.
